title: Dynamic Whole-Body Dancing with Humanoid Robots -- A Model-Based Control Approach
abstract: This paper presents an integrated model-based framework for generating and executing dynamic whole-body dance motions on humanoid robots. The framework operates in two stages: offline motion generation and online motion execution, both leveraging future state prediction to enable robust and dynamic dance motions in real-world environments. In the offline motion generation stage, human dance demonstrations are captured via a motion capture (MoCap) system, retargeted to the robot by solving a Quadratic Programming (QP) problem, and further refined using Trajectory Optimization (TO) to ensure dynamic feasibility. In the online motion execution stage, a centroidal dynamics-based Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework tracks the planned motions in real time and proactively adjusts swing foot placement to adapt to real world disturbances. We validate our framework on the full-size humanoid robot Kuavo 4Pro, demonstrating the dynamic dance motions both in simulation and in a four-minute live public performance with a team of four robots. Experimental results show that longer prediction horizons improve both motion expressiveness in planning and stability in execution.
summary:
# 论文总结：Dynamic Whole-Body Dancing with Humanoid Robots -- A Model-Based Control Approach

## 1. 核心创新点

论文提出了一个面向人形机器人动态全身舞蹈的两阶段 model-based 控制框架，将离线动作生成与在线动作执行串联起来，并在两个阶段都利用 future state prediction 以兼顾动作动态性与真实环境下的鲁棒执行。

## 2. 技术创新拆解

- **两阶段动态舞蹈生成与执行流程**：摘要显示，该方法先基于人类舞蹈示例进行离线动作生成，再进行在线实时执行：先用 MoCap 获取人类舞蹈演示，通过求解 QP 完成向机器人本体的 retargeting，并使用 Trajectory Optimization 提升动态可行性；随后在执行阶段用基于 centroidal dynamics 的 MPC 对规划轨迹进行实时跟踪。
- **面向扰动的在线自适应执行机制**：摘要表明，在线控制部分不仅跟踪预先规划的动作，还会主动调整 swing foot placement 来应对真实环境中的扰动；同时，作者强调更长的 prediction horizon 有助于同时提升规划阶段的动作表现力与执行阶段的稳定性。

## 3. 方法论突破

- **新颖性**：新意在于将 MoCap-retargeting、Trajectory Optimization 与 centroidal dynamics-based MPC 统一到同一套舞蹈控制流程中，并把 future state prediction 同时用于离线规划和在线执行，而不是仅做单阶段动作回放或局部跟踪。
- **关键技术**：关键技术包括：基于 MoCap 的人类舞蹈采集、通过 Quadratic Programming 完成机器人重定向、通过 Trajectory Optimization 保证动态可行性，以及基于 centroidal dynamics 的 Model Predictive Control 结合 swing foot placement 调整来实现实时稳定执行。
- **理论支撑**：摘要未披露具体理论证明；仅说明方法建立在 centroidal dynamics、QP、Trajectory Optimization 与 MPC 等模型化控制工具之上，但没有给出定理、收敛性分析或稳定性证明细节。

## 4. 实验验证

- **主要基准/场景**：在全尺寸人形机器人 Kuavo 4Pro 上进行了验证，包含仿真测试与一次由四台机器人参与的四分钟现场公开表演；摘要未披露具体 benchmark 名称或标准数据集。
- **性能提升**：摘要给出的结论是：更长的 prediction horizon 能改善规划时的动作表现力，并提升执行时的稳定性；摘要未披露具体数值提升、统计显著性或定量评价指标。
- **消融实验**：摘要未披露具体消融细节；仅可看出作者比较了不同 prediction horizon 对表现力与稳定性的影响，但未说明实验设置、对照项、指标或完整消融结果。

## 5. 局限与启发

- **当前局限**：从摘要可见，该框架依赖 MoCap 人类舞蹈演示、机器人模型以及较完整的离线到在线控制链路；但摘要未说明其在不同舞种、不同地面条件、强扰动场景或长时连续运行中的边界表现。
- **未来方向**：合理的后续方向包括在更多机器人平台、更多舞蹈风格和更标准化评测协议下做系统验证，并进一步公开定量指标、失败案例与消融分析；这些内容摘要中尚未展开。
- **可迁移性**：从方法描述看，该框架具有迁移到其他人形机器人平台的潜力，因为其核心依赖于 retargeting、动力学建模与 MPC；但摘要未披露跨平台适配成本、参数重整流程或在不同硬件上的泛化证据。

## 6. 一句话总结

这项工作提出了一个把离线动作生成与在线预测控制结合起来的人形机器人全身舞蹈框架，并在 Kuavo 4Pro 上通过仿真和公开表演展示了其动态执行能力，但摘要未提供详细 benchmark、数值结果、系统化消融或理论证明。

recognized_fields.title: Dynamic Whole-Body Dancing with Humanoid Robots -- A Model-Based Control Approach
recognized_fields.abstract: This paper presents an integrated model-based framework for generating and executing dynamic whole-body dance motions on humanoid robots. The framework operates in two stages: offline motion generation and online motion execution, both leveraging future state prediction to enable robust and dynamic dance motions in real-world environments. In the offline motion generation stage, human dance demonstrations are captured via a motion capture (MoCap) system, retargeted to the robot by solving a Quadratic Programming (QP) problem, and further refined using Trajectory Optimization (TO) to ensure dynamic feasibility. In the online motion execution stage, a centroidal dynamics-based Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework tracks the planned motions in real time and proactively adjusts swing foot placement to adapt to real world disturbances. We validate our framework on the full-size humanoid robot Kuavo 4Pro, demonstrating the dynamic dance motions both in simulation and in a four-minute live public performance with a team of four robots. Experimental results show that longer prediction horizons improve both motion expressiveness in planning and stability in execution.

generator_input.note: Generated materials use the parsed paper.md content as source input.
